summary: READY as a deferred composition family. Both realizations preserve the shared-trunk late-branch portfolio mechanism and stay honestly blocked on new branch-resume plus portfolio-accounting primitives.

realizations.title: Online Resume Tree Portfolio
realizations.mechanism_preservation: Preserves the family by making the searchable object a shared-trunk candidate that forks multiple late finisher continuations from one frozen trunk checkpoint and wins only if the portfolio beats single-path controls under shared-compute accounting.
realizations.instructions: Add a portfolio-candidate primitive to the harness: one trunk run on the frozen pr1394 base saves a designated late checkpoint, then the candidate fans out 2 branch continuations from that checkpoint inside the same execution record. Keep tokenizer, architecture, optimizer stack, dataset contract, and downstream metric fixed to the published pr1394 base. Use 2 controls and up to 4 novelty candidates per executable pack. Controls are two single-path continuations from the same checkpoint with the default late finisher/export path, duplicated to measure control spread. Each novelty candidate is one portfolio object with exactly 2 branches, equal per-branch continuation budget, and branch definitions sourced from already-supported late policies rather than fresh patch bundles. Score the candidate by the best downstream `quantized_sliding_window` `score_bpb` among its branches, while charging the shared trunk once and both branch continuations in full. Admit only if the best branch clears both controls by more than the observed control band and the portfolio uses less total continuation compute than training the same two finishers as independent full runs.
control_plan: Freeze `frontier_rebase/pr1394/train_gpt_human.py` and the published SP8192/GPTQ-embeddings/depth-recurrence/MuonEq-R/SDClip stack for every branch and control.
Use one late trunk checkpoint window chosen before the pack starts; do not let candidates pick different trunk histories.
Run 2 controls as duplicated default single-path continuations from that same checkpoint to estimate pack-local noise.
Materialize up to 4 novelty portfolio candidates, each containing exactly 2 branches with equal post-fork budget.
admission_rules: A realization is positive only if the best branch in the portfolio beats both controls on downstream `score_bpb` beyond control spread.
Reject candidates whose apparent win exists only on pre-quant `val_bpb` or final-step ranking.
Reject candidates if shared-trunk accounting shows no compute advantage over training the same branch policies as separate full runs.
Do not admit branch menus that differ only by scalar threshold nudges; each branch must encode a distinct late-policy program.
metric_plan: Primary metric: best branch downstream `score_bpb` from `quantized_sliding_window`.
Secondary diagnostics: branch-level pre-quant `val_bpb`, branch-to-branch spread, and wallclock/GPU-seconds consumed after the fork.
Tie-break only on artifact size after downstream and pre-quant evidence agree.
Log both portfolio-level score and per-branch scores so the mechanism remains falsifiable.
implementation_notes: New primitive required: resume a saved checkpoint into multiple child continuations linked under one candidate id.
New primitive required: portfolio-aware scoring that groups child runs, charges shared trunk cost once, and records branch-level artifacts.
Persist `portfolio_group_id`, `trunk_checkpoint_id`, branch configs, branch budgets, and downstream metrics in the run summary.
Keep branch definitions narrow: late-policy differences only, no tokenizer/base/architecture drift, and no hidden composition with unrelated top-level families.

realizations.title: Checkpoint Window Fan-Out Portfolio
realizations.mechanism_preservation: Preserves the family by keeping the candidate as a shared-trunk portfolio, but realizes diversity through branching across a bounded late checkpoint window rather than through two different branch policies from one checkpoint.
realizations.instructions: Add a grouped fan-out primitive to the harness: one trunk run emits a small fixed window of adjacent late checkpoints, and each candidate branches from 2 or 3 of those checkpoints with the same micro-finisher template and equal post-fork budget. This stays a branch-portfolio family because the object being judged is the pooled late portfolio built from one shared trunk, not a single checkpoint selector. Keep the frozen pr1394 base unchanged. Use 2 duplicated single-path controls from the latest checkpoint under the default finisher/export path, plus up to 4 novelty portfolio candidates. Each novelty candidate specifies a checkpoint subset, one shared branch template, equal budget per branch, and a portfolio aggregation rule of best downstream branch score under shared-trunk cost accounting. Admit only if the portfolio beats the control band and also shows value that cannot be reduced to a trivial single best checkpoint picked after the fact.
control_plan: Freeze the same trunk training trace and checkpoint-emission schedule for every candidate in the pack.
Emit a bounded late checkpoint window, for example 3 adjacent checkpoints in the last training segment; do not let candidates widen the window in the first probe.
Run 2 duplicated controls as default single-path continuations from the latest checkpoint to establish the noise band.
Run up to 4 novelty portfolio candidates, each branching from 2 or 3 checkpoints in that fixed window with equal continuation budget and one common branch template.
admission_rules: A realization is positive only if the portfolio best branch beats both duplicated controls on downstream `score_bpb` beyond control spread.
Retire the realization if the winner is always identical to the last checkpoint and the portfolio adds no measurable benefit over a single-path continuation.
Retire the realization if the apparent gain comes from ex post checkpoint cherry-picking without shared-compute advantage.
Do not vary checkpoint spacing or branch count as free thresholds in round one; keep the checkpoint window fixed and test the topology first.
metric_plan: Primary metric: portfolio best downstream `score_bpb` from `quantized_sliding_window`.
Secondary diagnostics: per-checkpoint branch downstream scores, pre-quant `val_bpb`, branch failure rate, and shared-trunk versus independent-run compute accounting.
Record whether the winning branch came from an earlier checkpoint or the latest checkpoint to distinguish real portfolio value from selector collapse.
Use artifact size only as a final tie-breaker.
implementation_notes: New primitive required: emit and retain a bounded late checkpoint window from one trunk run, then schedule grouped child continuations from each checkpoint.
New primitive required: portfolio aggregator that links child runs by `portfolio_group_id`, computes best-branch score, and logs shared trunk cost separately from child continuation cost.
Add a verifier field that marks whether the portfolio result collapses to a plain checkpoint selector; if so, retire or reclassify rather than overclaiming a branch-topology win.
Keep the branch template fixed across checkpoints in this realization so the executable difference is checkpoint-window fan-out, not mixed branch-policy tuning.
